perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information and data of a confidential nature belonging to the disclosing party, its affiliates and/or licensors ("Discloser"), including without limitation, proprietary, technical, developmental, marketing, sales, operating, financial, performance, cost, business and process information and plans, software, and computer programming techniques which are disclosed or made available pursuant to this Agreement in connection with the Evaluation.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information and data of a confidential nature belonging to the revealing party, its affiliates and/or licensors ("Discloser"), including without limitation, proprietary, technical, developmental, marketing, sales, operating, financial, performance, cost, business and process information and plans, software, and computer programming techniques which are disclosed or made available pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is modified by removing the phrase 'in connection with the Evaluation'. Now, it's unclear whether the confidential information must be tied to the evaluation to be considered confidential, thus broadening the scope and contradicting what the original text was intended, by broadening the scope it creates uncertainty in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined Confidential Information as being tied to the Evaluation. The changed text removes this limitation. The Contradicted Text states the Confidential Information should only be used for the purposes of discussions relating to the Evaluation. Removing 'in connection with the Evaluation' broadens the scope of 'Confidential Information' and makes it unclear whether it needs to be tied to the Evaluation. Thus, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text because it creates uncertainty about what information is considered confidential and whether it can be used outside the evaluation, which directly conflicts with the clause that restricts its use to evaluation-related discussions, thus a strong in-text contradiction is introduced

perturbation.original_text: Except for the software products and GO data, if any, disclosed hereunder, Confidential Information shall not include information which (a) is known to the party receiving the information from Discloser ("Recipient") at the time of disclosure and is not subject to restriction; (b) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the Recipient by publication, commercial use or otherwise through no fault of Recipient; (c) is lawfully obtained from a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient without access to the Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Except for the software products and GO data, if any, disclosed hereunder, Proprietary Information shall not include information which (a) is known to the party receiving the information from Discloser ("Recipient") at the time of disclosure and is not subject to restriction; (b) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the Recipient by publication, commercial use or otherwise through no fault of Recipient; (c) is lawfully obtained from a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient without access to the Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' introduces a new term without defining its relationship to 'Confidential Information.' This is an in-text contradiction because the agreement operates on the definition and usage of "Confidential Information". Making this change mid-agreement creates uncertainty. Whether 'Proprietary Information' shares the same scope, it's broader, or completely unrelated is now ambiguous and may lead to uncertainty in the application of the rest of the agreement's clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' without defining the relationship between the two creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text relies specifically on the term 'Confidential Information' for setting obligations regarding use, disclosure, and maintenance. By introducing 'Proprietary Information', the agreement now lacks clarity on whether these obligations apply to it, causing ambiguity and potential unenforceability of the contradicted clause. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Protected Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' creates ambiguity because 'Protected Information' is undefined. This introduces an in-text contradiction as it's unclear if 'Protected Information' is the same as, a subset of, or different from 'Confidential Information' as previously defined, making it vague and hard to understand what the term 'Protected Information' entails.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Protected Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' introduces a contradiction. The Contradicted Text references both 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information', but the contract only defines 'Confidential Information'. The change in the Changed Text leaves the meaning of 'Protected Information' undefined, while the Contradicted Text uses 'Protected Information' as if it has a defined meaning, leading to confusion and uncertainty about the parties' obligations regarding 'Protected Information'. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Data; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' mid-sentence without defining 'Confidential Data' creates an internal inconsistency. While conceptually similar, the legal agreement now implies two potentially distinct types of confidential material, generating an in-text contradiction and making this section ambiguous. It's uncertain whether this change alters the types of information the agreement protects.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Data; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' mid-sentence. The contradicted text mentions both 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' in the same paragraph, but it's now unclear if they are the same thing or two distinct categories, creating a contradiction because the obligations related to each are not clearly defined or differentiated after the change. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, as the interchangeable use of terms without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations and ambiguity.
